page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local NVMe storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2056.0 and strong single-threaded compression performance, with gzip compression at 33.85 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.22 GB/sec. Geekbench text processing also scores in the strong tier at 2412.0. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound workloads show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is poor at 2147.27, and the Geekbench multi-core score is weak at 2628.0. Memory performance is constrained, with PassMark memory scoring in the poor tier at 1238.89 and uncached large-block read bandwidth registering at a poor 19.96 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i-flex.large offers a modern DDR5 memory subsystem and Gen8 Intel Xeon architecture at a lower resource density, making it an entry-tier option for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution or high memory bandwidth. Tradeoffs include weak database operations (1140.31 on PassMark) and low Redis SET performance (3.76 million operations per second). It is suitable for single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight background utility jobs rather than memory-intensive databases or parallelized compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz under the Nitro hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs (1 physical core with hyper-threading), 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM at 7 GHz, and no local storage or GPU. The instance exhibits strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2056.0 and strong single-threaded compression benchmarks. However, multi-core and memory performance are limited, with stress-ng multi-core and uncached memory read bandwidth falling into poor performance tiers. Qualitatively, this instance provides a cost-effective entry point to modern Intel Xeon architecture for workloads with low resource density. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and basic web serving, while intensive database operations and parallel computing tasks are constrained by its hardware profile.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor.
tagline: A general-purpose x86_64 virtual machine featuring DDR5 memory and strong single-threaded performance for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 2 vCPUs
8.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression
Limited multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight web serving
